intro: Papaver impeditum, also known as the Armenian poppy, is a species of poppy that is native to the mountainous regions of Armenia and Turkey. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory effects. Bioactive constituents of Papaver impeditum include alkaloids such as papaverine and noscapine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with Papaver impeditum include tinctures, infusions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and alleviate respiratory discomfort.
